name: Rise of the Runelords
subtitle: Anniversary Edition
description: In the sleepy coastal town of Sandpoint, evil is brewing. An attack by crazed goblins reveals the shadows of a forgotten past returning to threaten the town—and perhaps all of Varisia.
_startPathDoc: Bug #39 (2026-04-17): Canonical opener for RotR is the Swallowtail Festival at Sandpoint's Cathedral Square (in front of the new cathedral), NOT the town's generic Main Road hub. startPath is consumed by worldTreeMigration to plant the party at Cathedral Square on a fresh campaign start, bypassing the defaultEntry cascade that would otherwise route them to Main Road. Each entry matches a node NAME in the world tree seed (case-insensitive). Bug #68 (2026-04-18): renamed 'Market Square' → 'Cathedral Square' in both the seed and here so the tree node and the chapter narrative agree on the festival plaza's name (prevents extractor from creating a duplicate ghost node).

chapters.name: Burnt Offerings
chapters.synopsis: The PCs attend the Swallowtail Festival and defend Sandpoint from a goblin raid. They uncover treachery at the Sandpoint Glassworks, explore ancient catacombs below the town, and assault Thistletop to confront the woman whose madness threatens all of Varisia.

milestones.trigger: After clearing the Glassworks, before entering Catacombs of Wrath
Early in exploration of Thistletop, or at climax of Catacombs

parts.name: Festival and Fire
parts.description: The Swallowtail Festival at Sandpoint's new cathedral turns deadly when goblins attack the town.

encounters.name: Initial Assault
encounters.description: As Father Zantus begins the consecration speech, a thunderstone crack signals the goblin attack. Three goblins emerge from hiding to attack the festival-goers.
encounters.readAloud: A sharp retort, like the crack of distant thunder, slices through the excited crowd. A woman's scream slices through the air, then another. Beyond them, a sudden surge of strange new voices rises—high-pitched, tittering shrieks that sound not quite human. The crowd parts and something low to the ground races by, giggling with disturbing glee.
encounters.tactics: Goblins fight erratically—one climbs a table for higher ground, another gets distracted by food, a third grabs a carving knife.

encounters.name: Goblin Pyros
encounters.description: Goblins have found a cart with fuel for the bonfire and are lighting everything on fire. A goblin warchanter leads the group.
encounters.readAloud: A terrified scream comes from the north, where a cart laden with fuel for the evening bonfire has been set ablaze by a group of goblins. They dance and cackle around the blazing cart as townspeople flee in terror.

enemies.name: Goblin Warchanter
enemies.special: Inspire Courage +1

encounters.tactics: The warchanter sings to inspire her allies while the warriors throw torches and attack. If the warchanter falls, goblins make morale checks.

encounters.name: Die, Dog, Die!
encounters.description: Near the White Deer tavern, a noble is cornered by a goblin commando mounted on a goblin dog. His hunting dog lies dead nearby.
encounters.readAloud: A man cries out from near the White Deer—a well-dressed nobleman is pinned against the wall, a whimpering dog at his feet. A goblin mounted on a snarling, mangy canine-like beast raises its horsechopper high for the killing blow.

enemies.name: Goblin Commando
enemies.special: Mounted on goblin dog

enemies.name: Goblin Dog
enemies.special: Allergic Reaction DC 12

encounters.tactics: The commando rides his goblin dog and charges with his horsechopper. Warriors protect their leader.
encounters.storyNote: Saving Aldern Foxglove earns the PCs a grateful noble ally who becomes important later in the campaign.

events.name: The Swallowtail Festival
events.description: Festival games, welcoming speeches by Mayor Deverin and Sheriff Hemlock, a butterfly release at noon, free lunch from local taverns, and the consecration ceremony at sunset.

parts.name: Local Heroes
parts.description: In the aftermath of the goblin raid, the PCs become local celebrities. Several events and investigations unfold over the following days.

encounters.name: The Desecrated Vault
encounters.description: Sheriff Hemlock asks the PCs to investigate the town cemetery, where Father Tobyn's tomb has been broken into and his remains stolen.
encounters.storyNote: A robe of bones found nearby reveals someone animated the skeletons as a distraction while stealing Father Tobyn's remains.

encounters.name: The Shopkeep's Daughter
encounters.description: Shayliss Vinder lures a PC to the basement of the General Store on a pretense, but her father Ven Vinder arrives in a rage.
encounters.storyNote: A social encounter that can turn violent if handled poorly. Diplomacy or quick thinking can defuse the situation.

encounters.name: The Boar Hunt
encounters.description: Aldern Foxglove invites the PCs on a boar hunt in the Tickwood forest. They encounter a dangerous wild boar.

enemies.name: Wild Boar
enemies.special: Ferocity (fights below 0 HP)

encounters.storyNote: During the hunt, Aldern asks many personal questions, showing an unusual obsession with one of the PCs.

encounters.name: Monster in the Closet
encounters.description: A local boy named Aeren Barett claims there's a goblin hiding in his closet. His father thinks the boy is making it up, but the goblin is very real.

enemies.name: Goblin Commando
enemies.special: Starving and desperate

encounters.storyNote: This goblin survived the raid and has been hiding in the walls. Finding it reveals that goblins may have entered through the northern gate.

events.name: Local Fame
events.description: The PCs gain hero status in Sandpoint. Free rooms at the Rusty Dragon, discounts at shops, and admiring townsfolk follow them everywhere.

events.name: Ameiko's Request
events.description: Ameiko Kaijitsu, tavern owner and friend, goes missing after receiving a letter from her brother Tsuto asking her to meet at the Glassworks at midnight.

parts.name: Glass and Wrath
parts.description: Ameiko's disappearance leads the PCs to the Sandpoint Glassworks, where her brother Tsuto has murdered their father. Below lies an ancient catacomb.

encounters.name: Against the Goblins (Glassworks)
encounters.description: The Glassworks has been taken over by goblins loyal to Tsuto. Inside, they find the murdered body of Lonjiku Kaijitsu encased in molten glass.
encounters.readAloud: The furnaces burn bright in the Glassworks, but it's not glass being worked. The goblins have overrun the building, and amid the wreckage you can see a horrifying sight—a man's body, seated in a chair, his entire form covered in hardened sheets of glass.

encounters.name: Tsuto Kaijitsu
encounters.description: Tsuto, a half-elf monk, fights to escape. His journal reveals plans for a larger goblin assault and his obsession with Nualia.

enemies.name: Tsuto Kaijitsu
enemies.special: Monk abilities, flurry of blows, stunning fist DC 14

items: Potion of Cure Light Wounds x3

encounters.storyNote: Tsuto's journal reveals Nualia's plans: she intends to burn Sandpoint as an offering to Lamashtu and free a barghest named Malfeshnekor from beneath Thistletop.

encounters.name: B1: Guard Cave
encounters.description: The entrance to the Catacombs of Wrath is guarded by a sinspawn—a twisted creature born of ancient Thassilonian magic.

enemies.name: Sinspawn
enemies.special: Sin-linked, wrathful bite causes rage

encounters.name: B6: Ancient Prison
encounters.description: Wooden platforms over a deep pit. Sinspawn lurk in the cells of this ancient prison.

enemies.name: Sinspawn
enemies.special: Sin-linked

encounters.name: B4: Washing Pool
encounters.description: A vargouille lurks in this room near a pool of murky water.

enemies.name: Vargouille
enemies.special: Shriek (DC 12 Fort), Kiss (transforms victim)

encounters.name: B9: Prisoner Pits
encounters.description: Zombies trapped in pits, left over from ancient Thassilonian experiments.

enemies.name: Zombie
enemies.special: Staggered (single action per round)

encounters.storyNote: These zombies are harmless as long as no one enters the pits.

encounters.name: B13: Cathedral of Wrath
encounters.description: The heart of the catacombs—a cathedral to Lamashtu. The quasit Erylium guards the minor runewell of wrath, which can spawn sinspawn.
encounters.readAloud: An immense cathedral opens before you. Walls are covered in carvings depicting monstrous births and terrible acts of wrath. A glowing pool of orange liquid shimmers at the room's center, and hovering above it is a tiny, bat-winged female figure.

enemies.name: Erylium
enemies.special: Quasit demon, DR 5/cold iron, fly, invisibility, cause fear, summon sinspawn from runewell

encounters.storyNote: Destroying the runewell requires pouring holy water into it. Erylium can summon sinspawn (7 charges remain) from the pool.

parts.name: Thistletop
parts.description: The PCs assault Thistletop, the island fortress of the Thistletop goblin tribe, to stop Nualia and her allies before they can launch a second, devastating attack on Sandpoint.

encounters.name: Thistle Tunnels - Goblin Dogs
encounters.description: Dense thistle tunnels form a maze before the bridge to Thistletop. Goblin dogs patrol the tunnels.

enemies.name: Goblin Dog
enemies.special: Allergic Reaction DC 12

encounters.name: Gogmurt the Druid
encounters.description: Gogmurt, the Thistletop goblin druid, lurks in the thistle maze with his fire pelt cougar companion.

enemies.name: Gogmurt
enemies.special: Druid 4, animal companion, spells: flame blade, produce flame, entangle

encounters.storyNote: Gogmurt can be persuaded to help the PCs (DC 25 Diplomacy). He resents Nualia's takeover of the tribe.

encounters.name: Thistletop Ground Floor
encounters.description: The wooden fortress atop the island head is crawling with goblins. Warchief Ripnugget sits on his throne with his giant gecko mount Stickfoot.

enemies.name: Warchief Ripnugget
enemies.special: Fighter 3, mounted on giant gecko, Power Attack

enemies.name: Stickfoot (Giant Gecko)
enemies.special: Wall climbing

enemies.name: Goblin Warchanter
enemies.special: Inspire Courage +1

encounters.name: Nualia's Chambers
encounters.description: The dungeon level beneath Thistletop. Nualia, the fallen aasimar cleric, makes her stand here with her yeth hound guardians.
encounters.readAloud: The chamber is lit by guttering candles arranged around a dark altar to Lamashtu. A beautiful woman stands before it—yet her left hand ends not in fingers but in a demonic claw of red, twisted flesh. She turns, eyes blazing with unholy fury.

enemies.name: Nualia
enemies.special: Cleric 5, channel negative energy, spells, demonic left hand grants +2 STR

enemies.name: Yeth Hound
enemies.special: Bay (DC 12 Will or panicked), DR 5/silver, fly

encounters.storyNote: Nualia's journal and the Sihedron Medallion connect to the larger Runelords conspiracy.

encounters.name: Malfeshnekor
encounters.description: Deep below Thistletop, the barghest Malfeshnekor has been imprisoned for thousands of years. This is an optional but very dangerous encounter.

enemies.name: Malfeshnekor
enemies.special: Greater barghest, DR 10/magic, feed ability, blink, levitate, dimension door

encounters.storyNote: Optional boss. Extremely dangerous for level 3-4 PCs. Defeating Malfeshnekor prevents Nualia's larger plan.

npcs.name: Ameiko Kaijitsu
npcs.description: Half-elf owner of the Rusty Dragon tavern. Former adventurer, provides the PCs with free room and board.

npcs.name: Sheriff Belor Hemlock
npcs.description: Shoanti man, grim and dutiful. Sandpoint's sheriff who coordinates with the PCs after the goblin attack.

npcs.name: Father Abstalar Zantus
npcs.description: Sandpoint's religious leader. Kind and wise, provides healing and guidance.

npcs.name: Mayor Kendra Deverin
npcs.description: Friendly and approachable mayor of Sandpoint.

npcs.name: Shalelu Andosana
npcs.description: Elf ranger who patrols the hinterlands. Expert on goblin tribes and their recent unusual cooperation.

npcs.name: Aldern Foxglove
npcs.description: Charismatic Magnimarian noble saved during the goblin attack. Becomes obsessed with a PC.

npcs.name: Nualia Tobyn
npcs.description: Fallen aasimar, daughter of the late Father Tobyn. Serves Lamashtu and orchestrated the goblin attacks.

npcs.name: Tsuto Kaijitsu
npcs.description: Half-elf monk, Ameiko's brother. In love with Nualia, betrayed his family and Sandpoint.

npcs.name: Erylium
npcs.description: Ancient quasit demon guarding the Catacombs of Wrath beneath Sandpoint.

locations.name: Sandpoint
locations.description: Sleepy coastal town in Varisia. Population ~1,200. Known for its cathedral, Glassworks, and friendly residents.

locations.name: Sandpoint Cathedral
locations.description: Newly rebuilt multi-faith cathedral. Site of the Swallowtail Festival.

locations.name: Rusty Dragon Tavern
locations.description: Best tavern in Sandpoint, run by Ameiko Kaijitsu. PCs' home base.

locations.name: Sandpoint Glassworks
locations.description: Glass factory owned by the Kaijitsu family. Tsuto used it as his base for the goblin raids.

locations.name: Catacombs of Wrath
locations.description: Ancient Thassilonian ruins beneath Sandpoint. Contains the minor runewell of wrath.

locations.name: Thistletop
locations.description: Island fortress of the Thistletop goblin tribe. Built on a stone head-shaped island.

chapters.name: The Skinsaw Murders
chapters.synopsis: A series of gruesome murders terrorizes Sandpoint and Magnimar. The PCs must track a ghoulish serial killer through a haunted manor house and into the secret lair of a murderous cult.

milestones.trigger: After investigating the Hambley Farm murders

parts.name: Murder Most Foul
parts.description: Grisly murders at the Sandpoint lumber mill draw the PCs into an investigation. A Sihedron star has been carved into each victim.

encounters.name: Habe's Sanitorium
encounters.description: The PCs investigate the sanitorium where a suspect is being held, discovering the doctor has dark secrets.

enemies.name: Caizarlu Zerren
enemies.special: Necromancer 4, spells: vampiric touch, ray of enfeeblement, command undead

parts.name: The Thing in the Attic
parts.description: The murders escalate as undead scarecrows terrorize the Hambley farmlands south of Sandpoint.

encounters.name: The Hambley Farm
encounters.description: The farmlands are infested with ghouls disguised as scarecrows. The Skinsaw Man has turned the farmers into undead.

enemies.name: Ghoul Scarecrow
enemies.special: Paralysis (DC 13 Fort)

parts.name: Foxglove Manor - The Misgivings
parts.description: The PCs explore the haunted Foxglove Manor, confronting ghostly visions and undead horrors to face the Skinsaw Man himself—Aldern Foxglove, now a ghast.

encounters.name: The Haunted Manor
encounters.description: Foxglove Manor is filled with haunts—ghostly manifestations that affect the PCs with visions of the Foxglove family's tragic history. Each haunt targets a different save.

enemies.name: Haunt (Various)
enemies.special: Will/Fort saves DC 12-16. Destroyed by channeling positive energy or specific actions.

encounters.storyNote: Haunts reveal the Foxglove family history: Vorel's lichdom attempt, Kasanda's murder, Traver's suicide, Cyralie's immolation.

encounters.name: Iesha's Prison
encounters.description: Iesha Foxglove, Aldern's murdered wife, has risen as a revenant. If released, she will lead the PCs to her killer.

enemies.name: Iesha Foxglove (Revenant)
enemies.special: DR 5/slashing, focused on killing Aldern. Will not attack PCs unless provoked.

encounters.storyNote: Iesha can be an ally—she leads directly to Aldern if the PCs don't attack her.

encounters.name: The Skinsaw Man
encounters.description: In the caverns beneath the manor, Aldern Foxglove—now the ghast known as the Skinsaw Man—awaits, surrounded by his ghoul minions.

enemies.name: The Skinsaw Man (Aldern Foxglove)
enemies.special: Ghast lord, sneak attack +2d6, obsession with a PC, stalker abilities

enemies.name: Ghoul
enemies.special: Paralysis DC 13

encounters.storyNote: Aldern's obsession with one PC makes this deeply personal. His journal and effects lead to the Skinsaw Cult in Magnimar.

parts.name: Shadows of Time
parts.description: The trail leads to Magnimar, where the PCs uncover the Skinsaw Cult operating beneath the city's clock tower.

encounters.name: The Shadow Clock
encounters.description: The PCs must climb the ancient Shadow Clock tower in Magnimar's Underbridge district to confront the cult leader, Justice Ironbriar.

enemies.name: Justice Ironbriar
enemies.special: Elf rogue 3/cleric 4, sneak attack, spells, Skinsaw Cult leader

enemies.name: Skinsaw Cultist
enemies.special: Skinsaw masks, sneak attack +1d6

enemies.name: Xanesha
enemies.special: Lamia matriarch, spells, Impaler of Thorns spear, mask of giants. Final boss of chapter.

encounters.storyNote: Xanesha is the true mastermind. Her defeat reveals connections to her sister Lucrecia at Fort Rannick.

npcs.name: Aldern Foxglove / The Skinsaw Man
npcs.description: The noble saved in Chapter 1 has become a ghast serial killer, obsessed with one of the PCs.

npcs.name: Xanesha
npcs.description: Lamia matriarch manipulating the Skinsaw Cult to harvest greedy souls for Karzoug.

npcs.name: Justice Ironbriar
npcs.description: Corrupt elf judge who leads the Skinsaw Cult in Magnimar.

locations.name: Foxglove Manor (The Misgivings)
locations.description: Haunted manor south of Sandpoint. Three generations of Foxglove tragedy haunt its halls.

locations.name: Magnimar
locations.description: Major city in Varisia. Home to the Skinsaw Cult's operations.

locations.name: The Shadow Clock
locations.description: Crumbling clock tower in Magnimar's Underbridge district. Skinsaw Cult headquarters.

chapters.name: The Hook Mountain Massacre
chapters.synopsis: The PCs travel to Fort Rannick to investigate the disappearance of the Black Arrows rangers, discovering an ogre invasion and a sinister plot involving Lucrecia, Xanesha's sister.

milestones.trigger: After the dam at Skull's Crossing

parts.name: In the Hook's Shadow
parts.description: The PCs discover Fort Rannick has fallen to the Kreeg ogre clan. Surviving Black Arrows need help retaking their fortress.

encounters.name: Retaking Fort Rannick
encounters.description: The PCs assault Fort Rannick to drive out the ogres. Multiple approaches possible—frontal assault, secret tunnels, or stealth.

enemies.name: Jaagrath Kreeg
enemies.special: Ogre fighter 4, leader of the Kreeg clan forces at the fort

parts.name: Skull's Crossing
parts.description: The ancient Thassilonian dam at Skull's Crossing is failing. If it breaks, Turtleback Ferry will be flooded.

encounters.name: Skull's Crossing
encounters.description: The dam is infested with ogres and a giant ettin. Deep within, ancient Thassilonian magic powers the floodgates.

enemies.name: Skull Ripper (Ettin)
enemies.special: Two heads, superior two-weapon fighting

parts.name: Harrowing the Hook
parts.description: The PCs ascend Hook Mountain to confront Barl Breakbones, the stone giant necromancer leading the Kreeg ogres, and Lucrecia the lamia.

encounters.name: The Kreeg Clanhold
encounters.description: The ogre clanhold atop Hook Mountain. Mama Kreeg rules here with her monstrous brood, and Barl Breakbones lurks in the depths.

enemies.name: Barl Breakbones
enemies.special: Stone giant necromancer, spells, animate dead, stone giant abilities

enemies.name: Lucrecia
enemies.special: Lamia matriarch, Xanesha's sister, wisdom drain, spells

encounters.storyNote: Barl's orders come from Mokmurian, a stone giant warlord amassing an army in the east.

npcs.name: Lucrecia
npcs.description: Lamia matriarch, sister of Xanesha. Manipulated the Kreegs and corrupted Fort Rannick.

npcs.name: Barl Breakbones
npcs.description: Stone giant necromancer serving Mokmurian.

npcs.name: Jakardros Sovark
npcs.description: Surviving Black Arrow ranger, helps retake Fort Rannick.

locations.name: Fort Rannick
locations.description: Border fortress of the Black Arrows, now occupied by ogres.

locations.name: Turtleback Ferry
locations.description: Small lakeside town threatened by the failing dam.

locations.name: Skull's Crossing
locations.description: Ancient Thassilonian dam holding back the Storval Deep.

locations.name: Hook Mountain Clanhold
locations.description: Ogre fortress high on Hook Mountain.

chapters.name: Fortress of the Stone Giants
chapters.synopsis: Stone giants attack Sandpoint under orders from the warlord Mokmurian. The PCs must defend the town, then travel to the ancient fortress of Jorgenfist to end the threat at its source.

milestones.trigger: After defending Sandpoint from the giant raid

parts.name: Stones Over Sandpoint
parts.description: A massive stone giant raid on Sandpoint. The PCs must defend the town and its citizens from the assault.

encounters.name: Giant Raid on Sandpoint
encounters.description: Stone giants and a dragon attack Sandpoint. The PCs must split up to defend multiple locations.

enemies.name: Young Red Dragon (Longtooth)
enemies.special: Breath weapon (40ft cone, 6d10 fire, DC 20 Ref)

encounters.storyNote: The real goal of the raid is a diversion—while the PCs fight giants, another team raids an underground library beneath Sandpoint.

parts.name: Journey to Jorgenfist
parts.description: The PCs travel east across Varisia to the ancient stone giant fortress of Jorgenfist in the Iron Peaks.

encounters.name: Jorgenfist - The Pit
encounters.description: The fortress is massive, guarded by stone giant sentries, dire bears, and enslaved hill giants.

encounters.name: Mokmurian
encounters.description: Deep in the library beneath Jorgenfist, Mokmurian—a stone giant wizard empowered by Karzoug's magic—makes his stand.

enemies.name: Mokmurian
enemies.special: Stone giant wizard 7, spells: cloudkill, wall of force, dimension door, fog cloud. Commands armies.

encounters.storyNote: Mokmurian's death triggers a message from Karzoug himself, who speaks through the corpse. The Runelord is awakening.

npcs.name: Mokmurian
npcs.description: Runty but brilliant stone giant wizard, empowered by Karzoug's magic. Commands the giant army.

npcs.name: Conna the Wise
npcs.description: Stone giant elder who opposes Mokmurian. Can help the PCs infiltrate Jorgenfist.

locations.name: Jorgenfist
locations.description: Ancient stone giant fortress in the Iron Peaks. Mokmurian's seat of power.

chapters.name: Sins of the Saviors
chapters.synopsis: The PCs seek the legendary Runeforge, a pocket dimension where ancient Thassilonian wizards once created weapons of incredible power. They must forge weapons capable of defeating a Runelord.

milestones.trigger: After exploring half of Runeforge's wings

parts.name: Into Runeforge
parts.description: The PCs travel to Rimeskull and unlock the entrance to Runeforge—a demiplane where the Runelords' servants still scheme after ten thousand years.

encounters.name: Halls of Wrath
encounters.description: The domain of Alaznist's followers, filled with evocation warriors and conjured fiends.

enemies.name: Warriors of Wrath
enemies.special: Fighter/wizard, fireball, shocking grasp

enemies.name: Highlady Athroxis
enemies.special: Wizard 13, evocation specialist, chain lightning, disintegrate, delayed blast fireball

encounters.name: Shimmering Veils of Pride
encounters.description: A mirror-filled domain of illusions, ruled by a mithral mage who traps visitors in reflections.

enemies.name: Delvahine
enemies.special: Succubus enchantress. Charm, dominate, energy drain

encounters.name: Forging Runeforged Weapons
encounters.description: The PCs must combine essences from at least two opposing Runeforge wings to create dominant weapons that can harm Karzoug.
encounters.storyNote: The pool at Runeforge's center can enchant weapons with Runeforged power. A weapon infused with opposing sin components (e.g., Wrath + Sloth) gains special abilities against Runelords.

npcs.name: Highlady Athroxis
npcs.description: Wizard of Wrath, still serving Alaznist after millennia in Runeforge.

npcs.name: Delvahine
npcs.description: Succubus ruler of the Shimmering Veils. May negotiate rather than fight.

locations.name: Runeforge
locations.description: Demiplane containing seven wings, each devoted to a school of Thassilonian sin magic.

locations.name: Rimeskull
locations.description: Frozen mountain peak where seven stone heads guard the entrance to Runeforge.

chapters.name: Spires of Xin-Shalast
chapters.synopsis: The PCs journey to the legendary lost city of Xin-Shalast, high in the Kodar Mountains, to confront the awakening Runelord Karzoug before he can return to full power.

milestones.trigger: After reaching the Pinnacle of Avarice

parts.name: Seeking Xin-Shalast
parts.description: The PCs must find the lost city of Xin-Shalast, hidden high in the Kodar Mountains. Ancient texts and ghostly guides lead the way.

encounters.name: Approach to Xin-Shalast
encounters.description: The mountain approaches are guarded by abominable snowmen and worse.

enemies.name: Abominable Snowman
enemies.special: Cold subtype, frightful gaze

parts.name: Xin-Shalast
parts.description: The ancient Thassilonian capital city, partially phased between dimensions. Lamia, giants, and worse patrol the crumbling streets.

encounters.name: Streets of Xin-Shalast
encounters.description: The city streets are patrolled by Karzoug's forces—stone giants, lamia, and wardens of rune.

enemies.name: Rune Giant
enemies.special: Rune-inscribed, command giants, spark shower, spell-like abilities

parts.name: The Pinnacle of Avarice
parts.description: The PCs ascend Karzoug's fortress—the Pinnacle of Avarice—to confront the Runelord himself before he can fully manifest.

encounters.name: Most High Ceoptra
encounters.description: The lamia priestess guarding the lower levels of the Pinnacle.

enemies.name: Most High Ceoptra
enemies.special: Lamia harridan, cleric spells, wisdom drain, unholy aura

encounters.name: Runelord Karzoug
encounters.description: The final battle. Karzoug the Claimer, Runelord of Greed, manifests in his runewell chamber, ready to unleash ten thousand years of accumulated power upon the world.
encounters.readAloud: The chamber blazes with golden light. Floating above a pool of molten gold, a towering figure materializes—robed in shimmering cloth of gold, bearing a burning glaive, his eyes alight with terrible intelligence and bottomless greed. Karzoug the Claimer has returned.

enemies.name: Karzoug the Claimer
enemies.special: Wizard 20, Runelord of Greed, time stop, meteor swarm, wish, prismatic wall, quickened spells. Vulnerable to Runeforged dominant weapons.

encounters.storyNote: Karzoug can only be permanently killed by Runeforged dominant weapons in his Runewell chamber. Without them, he regenerates. This is the climax of the entire campaign.

npcs.name: Karzoug the Claimer
npcs.description: Runelord of Greed, an ancient Thassilonian wizard-king awakening after 10,000 years. The ultimate antagonist of the campaign.

npcs.name: Most High Ceoptra
npcs.description: Lamia harridan high priestess serving Karzoug in the Pinnacle of Avarice.

locations.name: Xin-Shalast
locations.description: Ancient Thassilonian capital, hidden in the Kodar Mountains. Partially phased between dimensions.

locations.name: Pinnacle of Avarice
locations.description: Karzoug's fortress-temple atop Mhar Massif. The final dungeon of the campaign.
